choices.name: chc_ProceedToDelete
choices.choiceText: I understand I'm going to delete these Properties and would like to proceed

Flow.description: Screen flow to allow bulk uploading of Property Measure Costs
Flow.label: GMS - Bulk Property Cost Input

loops.name: Loop_Measures_into_Bulk_Input_Table
loops.label: Loop Measures into Bulk Input Table

fields.name: Commit_Screen_Header
fields.fieldText: The following Propery Measures will be updated

fields.name: Data_Table_Input_Header
fields.fieldText:
Input costs using table below

  • You can directly input costs in the table below
  • This table shows 1,500 records at a time
  • You can update a maximum of 200 records at a time
  • Please use the search box to filter
  • Tick the box at the top to choose all measures shown.

fields.name: Data_Table_Picker_Header
fields.fieldText:
Choose measures to export using table below


﻿

fields.name: EditSuccess
fields.fieldText: You have successfully edited {!var_Measure_Count} Property Measures

fields.name: Measure_Cost_Update_Count
fields.fieldText: Costs will be updated on {!Measure_Cost_Update.recordCount} measure records

textTemplates.name: txt_Bulk_Input_Guidance
textTemplates.text:
Guidance for bulk input measure cost data page 

Measure costs can be provided within the table individually, or by using the bulk upload function for multiple measures. (The table can be filtered by UMR number, measure type, or region to upload select measures at a time if required) 


Step 1: Access the measure cost table 

  • Once properties have been lodged through TrustMark, the associated measures will appear in the GRP measure cost table below. 
  • Only measures that do not yet have cost data entered will be displayed. 


Step 2: Copy the table into Excel 

  • Select and copy the table rows displayed on the GRP you want to enter measure cost data for. 
  • Paste the table into a blank Excel spreadsheet. 

Important: Do not delete, reorder, or rename any columns or column headings. 

 

Step 3: Enter measure cost data 

In the Excel spreadsheet: 

  • Locate the blank column titled “Total_Cost_of_Single_Measure_Installatio__c”. 
  • Enter the measure cost data into this column by either:  
  • Copying and pasting values from another spreadsheet, or 
  • Manually entering values row by row. 

Each measure is linked using the ID in the first column, so it is essential that this ID remains unchanged. 


Step 4: Review your data 

Before proceeding: 

  • Check that measure costs have only been entered into the “Total_Cost_of_Single_Measure_Installatio__c” column. 
  • Ensure no other values, IDs, or column headings have been amended. 

Do not amend any other fields, as this will prevent the measure costs from updating correctly in the GRP. 


Step 5: Upload the completed table 

  • Copy the completed rows from Excel. 
  • Paste it into the text box on the next page of the GRP form. 
  • Submit the form. 

Step 6: Confirmation after table input 

  • Once inputted, click next. 
  • The table will display all inputted measure costs for confirmation. 
  • Once checked, press next again.  

Step 7: Confirmation after submission 

  • Once submitted, measure costs will automatically update in the background. 
  • Measures that have had cost data successfully entered will be removed from the table view. 
  • The table will continue to display only measures without cost data. 

Viewing submitted measure costs 

Entered measure costs can be viewed at any time on the relevant property’s Property Details tab within the GRP.

textTemplates.name: txt_Table_Input_Guidance
textTemplates.text:
Guidance for bulk input measure cost data page 

Measure costs can be provided in the table below(The table can be filtered by UMR number, measure type, or region to upload select measures at a time if required) 


Step 1: Access the measure cost table 

  • Once properties have been lodged through TrustMark, the associated measures will appear in the GRP measure cost table below. 
  • Only measures that do not yet have cost data entered will be displayed. 


Step 3: Enter measure cost data 

In the table below: 

  • Enter the measure cost data into each record in the final column 

Step 6: Confirmation after table has been updated 

  • Once all required costs have been updated, click next. 
  • The table will display all inputted measure costs for confirmation. 
  • Once checked, press next again and then once more after the confirmation screen to return to the start of the form

Viewing submitted measure costs 

Entered measure costs can be viewed at any time on the relevant property’s Property Details tab within the GRP.
